title: Ferdinand Heilbuth

infobox.value: (27 June 1826, [Hamburg](/wiki/Hamburg "Hamburg") - 19 November 1889, [Paris](/wiki/Paris "Paris")) was a French painter; originally from Germany.

Arthur Baignières, "Société d'aquarellistes français", In: *Gazette des beaux-arts*, Paris, May 1879, pp.491-501 — [Online](https://gallica.bnf.fr/ark:/12148/bpt6k203108j/) @ [Gallica](/wiki/Gallica "Gallica").

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/08/Bingham_-_Ferdinand_Heilbuth_01.jpg/250px-Bingham_-_Ferdinand_Heilbuth_01.jpg)](/wiki/File:Bingham_-_Ferdinand_Heilbuth_01.jpg)

Ferdinand Heilbuth; by [Robert Jefferson Bingham](/wiki/Robert_Jefferson_Bingham "Robert Jefferson Bingham")

**Ferdinand Heilbuth** (27 June 1826, [Hamburg](/wiki/Hamburg "Hamburg") - 19 November 1889, [Paris](/wiki/Paris "Paris")) was a French painter; originally from Germany.

## Biography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ferdinand_Heilbuth&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Biography")]

His father, also named Ferdinand Heilbuth, was a [Rabbi](/wiki/Rabbi "Rabbi"). He initially pursued [rabbinical](/wiki/Rabbinical "Rabbinical") studies but, after a visit to [Antwerp](/wiki/Antwerp "Antwerp") in 1843, he decided to devote himself to art.[[1]](#cite_note-A-1)

He arrived in Paris in 1848 and enrolled at the workshop of [Paul Delaroche](/wiki/Paul_Delaroche "Paul Delaroche"), which was later taken over by [Charles Gleyre](/wiki/Charles_Gleyre "Charles Gleyre"). In 1850, finding himself penniless, he took up temporary residence with two political refugees from Germany. They shared a small apartment in [Montmartre](/wiki/Montmartre "Montmartre"). He had his first showing at the [Salon of 1853](/wiki/Salon_of_1853 "Salon of 1853").[[2]](#cite_note-O-2)

Between 1853 and 1855 he paid several visits to Rome.[[2]](#cite_note-O-2) At first, he focused on historical paintings, but soon switched to more colorful [genre](/wiki/Genre_art "Genre art") scenes. He was awarded medals at the [Salon of 1857](/wiki/Salon_of_1857 "Salon of 1857"), [Salon of 1859](/w/index.php?title=Salon_of_1859&action=edit&redlink=1 "Salon of 1859 (page does not exist)") and [Salon of 1861](/w/index.php?title=Salon_of_1861&action=edit&redlink=1 "Salon of 1861 (page does not exist)").[[1]](#cite_note-A-1) In 1863, his works were presented at the [Boston Athenæum](/wiki/Boston_Athen%C3%A6um "Boston Athenæum").

In 1870, during the [Franco-Prussian War](/wiki/Franco-Prussian_War "Franco-Prussian War"), he went to live in London.[[1]](#cite_note-A-1) There, he exhibited at the [Royal Academy](/wiki/Royal_Academy "Royal Academy") and the [Grosvenor Gallery](/wiki/Grosvenor_Gallery "Grosvenor Gallery").[[2]](#cite_note-O-2) He returned to Paris 1874 and became a [French citizen](/wiki/French_citizen "French citizen") in 1879. He was named an Officer in the [Legion of Honor](/wiki/Legion_of_Honor "Legion of Honor") in 1881.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

During that time, he began creating watercolors and became a founding member of the [Société d'aquarellistes français](/wiki/Soci%C3%A9t%C3%A9_d%27aquarellistes_fran%C3%A7ais "Société d'aquarellistes français").[[4]](#cite_note-4) He would also be increasingly influenced by [Impressionism](/wiki/Impressionism "Impressionism").

He died in 1889, at his mansion on the [Rue Ampère](/w/index.php?title=Rue_Amp%C3%A8re&action=edit&redlink=1 "Rue Ampère (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rue_Amp%C3%A8re "fr:Rue Ampère")], which had been built for him in 1882 by the architect [Gustave Adolphe Gerhardt](/w/index.php?title=Gustave_Adolphe_Gerhardt&action=edit&redlink=1 "Gustave Adolphe Gerhardt (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gustave_Adolphe_Gerhardt "fr:Gustave Adolphe Gerhardt")][[5]](#cite_note-5) He was interred at the [Cimetière du Père-Lachaise](/wiki/Cimeti%C3%A8re_du_P%C3%A8re-Lachaise "Cimetière du Père-Lachaise").
